channel.title: summarizedStoriesMainNLscienceAndDiscovery
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from summarizedStoriesMainNLscienceAndDiscovery, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: Scientists discover the master clock controlling biological growth
item.description: A newly discovered genetic clock acts as the master timekeeper for development, orchestrating bursts of gene activity.

item.title: Scientists crack an undruggable pancreatic cancer target, nearly doubling survival
item.description: A new drug, daraxonrasib, targets the KRAS protein, offering a breakthrough for pancreatic cancer treatment.

item.title: Goethe never knew this 40-million-year-old ant was hidden in his collection
item.description: Scientists examining amber from Goethe's personal collection discovered three hidden fossil insects, including an extinct ant preserved in extraordinary detail.

item.title: Beluga whales keep switching mates and it may be saving their species
item.description: Hidden beneath Arctic waters, beluga whales have long kept their family lives a mystery. By analyzing DNA from more than 600 belugas in Alaska's Bristol Bay, researchers are uncovering how their mating habits may help the species survive.
